events.description:
Hi there! We're Nate and Austin, and we love sharing this incredible home with others. But it's a small house and we're just two people with a limited schedule! We will do our best to accommodate as many visitors as we can and as often as we are able to ❤

Often considered to be one of his most elegant, the home was built in 1940 for local art professors Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler. Both women cared deeply about integrating art into the daily fabric of their own lives, and this becomes abundantly clear once you step foot inside the home.

During the tour you will learn all about the history of the home and the lives of its first owners. You will also have the opportunity to take photos both inside and out.

Want to experience the home more intimately? We also offer architecture enthusiasts the opportunity to stay overnight on Airbnb.

---

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!

events.description:
Join us for an art show and sale at Frank Lloyd Wright's Goetsch–Winckler house during the peak of the fall season! You'll be able to self tour the home and grounds and also browse a curated selection of original artwork by Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler—the home’s original owners—that will be available for purchase, offered in partnership with Triola’s Estate Sale Services.

Triola's owns the bulk of the former Michigan State University collection of Goetsch and Winckler's artwork, and will donate half of all proceeds during the art sale to the home, which will we then in turn use for various projects at the house as well as to purchase original artworks directly from Triola's to add to the home's archives.

There is only street parking available—please be courteous of our neighbors! Be aware that, depending on interest, the walk may be quite far. Shoe slip covers will be provided when indoors.

Commercial photography or videography is not allowed. Children under 13 are not permitted. Guests ages 13–17 must be accompanied by an adult.

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!

eventSeries.description:
Hi there! We're Nate and Austin, and we love sharing this incredible home with others. But it's a small house and we're just two people with a limited schedule! We will do our best to accommodate as many visitors as we can and as often as we are able to ❤

Often considered to be one of his most elegant, the home was built in 1940 for local art professors Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler. Both women cared deeply about integrating art into the daily fabric of their own lives, and this becomes abundantly clear once you step foot inside the home.

During the tour you will learn all about the history of the home and the lives of its first owners. You will also have the opportunity to take photos both inside and out.

Want to experience the home more intimately? We also offer architecture enthusiasts the opportunity to stay overnight on Airbnb.

---

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!

eventSeries.description:
Hi there! We're Nate and Austin, and we're the careful caretakers of the Goetsch–Winckler house. We absolutely love sharing this incredible home with others, and we hope you'll join us for this month's guided house tour!

Often considered to be one of Frank Lloyd Wright's most elegant, this inline Usonian home was built in 1940 for local art professors Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler. Both women cared deeply about integrating art into the daily fabric of their own lives, and this becomes abundantly clear once you step foot inside the home.

During the tour you will learn all about the history of the home and the lives of its first owners. You will also have the opportunity to take photos both inside and out.

Want to experience the home more intimately? We also offer architecture enthusiasts the opportunity to stay overnight on Airbnb.

Note: Tickets are available exactly 60 days before the home tour is scheduled. Tours have limited space and tickets tend to go fast, so mark your calendar if you'd like to book right when tickets become available!

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!

eventSeries.description:
Join us for an art show and sale at Frank Lloyd Wright's Goetsch–Winckler house during the peak of the fall season! You'll be able to self tour the home and grounds and also browse a curated selection of original artwork by Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler—the home’s original owners—that will be available for purchase, offered in partnership with Triola’s Estate Sale Services.

Triola's owns the bulk of the former Michigan State University collection of Goetsch and Winckler's artwork, and will donate half of all proceeds during the art sale to the home, which will we then in turn use for various projects at the house as well as to purchase original artworks directly from Triola's to add to the home's archives.

There is only street parking available—please be courteous of our neighbors! Be aware that, depending on interest, the walk may be quite far. Shoe slip covers will be provided when indoors.

Commercial photography or videography is not allowed. Children under 13 are not permitted. Guests ages 13–17 must be accompanied by an adult.

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!

eventSeries.description:
Hi there! We're Nate and Austin, and we're the careful caretakers of the Goetsch–Winckler house. We absolutely love sharing this incredible home with others, and we hope you'll join us for this month's guided house tour!

Often considered to be one of Frank Lloyd Wright's most elegant, this inline Usonian home was built in 1940 for local art professors Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler. Both women cared deeply about integrating art into the daily fabric of their own lives, and this becomes abundantly clear once you step foot inside the home.

During the tour you will learn all about the history of the home and the lives of its first owners. You will also have the opportunity to take photos both inside and out.

Want to experience the home more intimately? We also offer architecture enthusiasts the opportunity to stay overnight on Airbnb.

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!
eventSeries.name: Guided Tour of Frank Lloyd Wright’s Goetsch–Winckler House

eventSeries.description:
OKEMOS, MICHIGAN — Hi there! We're Nate and Austin, and we're the careful caretakers of the Goetsch–Winckler house. We absolutely love sharing this incredible home with others, and we hope you'll join us for this month's guided house tour!

Update Dec 2025: We're expecting our first child in April, so these are the only tour dates we can commit to at this time. You can probably expect more starting up in the fall, but we can’t make any promises right now ❤

Often considered to be one of Frank Lloyd Wright's most elegant, this inline Usonian home was built in 1940 for local art professors Alma Goetsch and Kathrine Winckler. Both women cared deeply about integrating art into the daily fabric of their own lives, and this becomes abundantly clear once you step foot inside the home.

During the tour you will learn all about the history of the home and the lives of its first owners. You will also have the opportunity to take photos both inside and out.

If you missed out on registration but still want to experience the home, you can check out our free 3D virtual tour! Want to experience the home even more intimately? We also offer architecture enthusiasts the opportunity to stay overnight on Airbnb.

PRIVATE PROPERTY. No trespassing. Please enjoy from a distance. Thank you!
